content: Laravel migration pattern for pivot/junction table: Schema::create('module_installations', function(Blueprint ) { ->id(); ->foreignId('tenant_id')->constrained('tenants')->onDelete('cascade'); ->foreignId('module_id')->constrained('modules')->onDelete('cascade'); ->string('status')->default('installed'); ->timestamp('installed_at')->nullable(); ->timestamps(); ->unique(['tenant_id','module_id']); }). For module_operations tracking, a separate module_operations table with status, action, message, timestamps provides atomic updates per-operation instead of JSON blob overwrites. Both tables live on the central connection since they track cross-tenant metadata.
